alkylation reagent: alkylated with 11 mM iodoacetamide for 15 min at room temperature in darkness.
alkylation concentration: alkylated with 11 mM iodoacetamide for 15 min at room temperature in darkness.
cleavage agent: trypsin was added at 1:50 trypsin-to-protein mass ratio for the first digestion overnight.
enrichment method: Kmal-modified peptides from the nuclear fraction were enriched with an anti-Kmal pan-antibody.
instrument: the peptides were subjected to NSI source followed by tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) in Q ExactiveTM Plus.
ionization type: the peptides were subjected to NSI source followed by tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) in Q ExactiveTM Plus.
reduction reagent: the protein solution was reduced with 5 mM dithiothreitol for 30 min at 56 °C.
reduction concentration: the protein solution was reduced with 5 mM dithiothreitol for 30 min at 56 °C.
